comparisons.midi_1_0: Variable-length byte stream (status + data bytes, running status)

comparisons.midi_2_0: Direct Registered/Assignable Controller messages (16384 banks × 128 controllers, 32-bit)
16-bit attribute on Note On/Off (e.g. articulation, pitch 7.9)
Is a real Note On (translation must convert)
JR Timestamps + JR Clock (1/31250 s resolution)
MIDI Clip File (SMF2CLIP), Container in development
USB-MIDI 2.0, Network MIDI 2.0 (UDP); UMP is transport-agnostic
MIDI 1.0 messages carried in UMP (MT 0x2); defined 1.0↔2.0 translation rules

translation_gotchas.description: 1.0 Note On velocity 0 → 2.0 Note Off. 2.0 Note On with 16-bit velocity that downscales to 0 must be sent as 1.0 velocity 1 (not 0, which would mean Note Off).
upscale with bit-repeat, downscale by truncation; center-based values scale around center (see doc `m2-115-u-midi-2-0-bit-scaling-and-resolution`).
2.0 Registered/Assignable Controllers map to 1.0 CC sequences (101/100 or 99/98, then 6/38); state machines are needed in the 1.0 direction.
UMP group information is lost when converting to a 1.0 byte stream (one group per stream).

practical_guidance: AKVJ uses the Web MIDI API, so everything on the wire is MIDI 1.0. MIDI 2.0 matters when: reading modern OS MIDI stacks' docs (they are UMP-native and translate), planning future-proof value handling (normalize to 0–1 floats internally), or reasoning about controllers that advertise MIDI 2.0 (they fall back to 1.0 over USB-MIDI 1.0/Web MIDI).
